title: cyathula prostrata var. achyranthoides
intro: Cyathula prostrata var. achyranthoides, commonly known as the creeping spikerush, is a type of plant in the family Cyathaceae that is native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The creeping spikerush has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The health benefits of Cyathula prostrata var. achyranthoides include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
